title: rosa canina var. sphaerica
intro: Rosa canina var. sphaerica, also known as the dwarf wild rose or alpine rose, is a compact and thorny shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina var. sphaerica have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
